title: podman remote-client connection fails to connect to lima-vm
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Following the instructions documented here [remote client](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/remote_client.md#using-the-client)  and podman-remote info hangs when run

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. First attempt was to run the exact command that the docs showed `podman-remote system connection add myuser --identity ~/.ssh/id_rsa ssh://192.168.122.1/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock`

2.  This resulted in a hang when I ran `podman-remote info`

> WUSTK185141-CV3:~ tk185141$ podman-remote info
> Error: failed to create sshClient: Connection to bastion host (ssh://tk185141@192.168.122.1:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) failed.: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 100.64.0.1:62596->192.168.122.1:22: read: connection reset by peer
> WUSTK185141-CV3:~ tk185141$ 
> 

3. I then went to the lima VM and attempted to verify where podman.sock was located
`tk185141@lima-default:/Users/tk185141$ ls /run/user/1640274643/podman/podman.sock 
/run/user/1640274643/podman/podman.sock
`
4.   This attempt also failed

> WUSTK185141-CV3:~ tk185141$ podman-remote system connection add myuser --identity ~/.ssh/id_rsa ssh://192.168.122.1/run/user/1640274643/podman/podman.sock
> WUSTK185141-CV3:~ tk185141$ podman system connection list
> Name        Identity                     URI
> myuser*     /Users/tk185141/.ssh/id_rsa  ssh://tk185141@192.168.122.1:22/run/user/1640274643/podman/podman.sock
> WUSTK185141-CV3:~ tk185141$ podman-remote info
> Error: failed to create sshClient: Connection to bastion host (ssh://tk185141@192.168.122.1:22/run/user/1640274643/podman/podman.sock) failed.: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 100.64.0.1:62692->192.168.122.1:22: read: connection reset by peer
> 

**Describe the results you received:**
**See above**

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected to get podman-remote info response

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Repeatedly

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Remote Client: 

> WUSTK185141-CV3:~ tk185141$ podman --version
> podman version 3.3.1
Lima VM:

> tk185141@lima-default:/Users/tk185141$ podman --version
> podman version 3.0.1


```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
tk185141@lima-default:/Users/tk185141$ podman info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.19.6
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.25, commit: unknown'
  cpus: 4
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "21.04"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: lima-default
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1640274643
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.11.0-31-generic
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 120377344
  memTotal: 4111007744
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0~rc95-0ubuntu1~21.04.2
      spec: 1.0.2-dev
      go: go1.16.2
      libseccomp: 2.5.1
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1640274643/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    selinuxEnabled: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/local/bin/slirp4netns
    package: Unknown
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.12
      commit: 7a104a101aa3278a2152351a082a6df71f57c9a3
      libslirp: 4.6.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.3.3
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 10h 29m 9.03s (Approximately 0.42 days)
registries:
  localhost:5000:
    Blocked: false
    Insecure: true
    Location: localhost:5000
    MirrorByDigestOnly: false
    Mirrors: null
    Prefix: localhost:5000
store:
  configFile: /home/tk185141.linux/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/local/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: Unknown
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.10.2
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.7.1
        FUSE library version 3.4.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.27
  graphRoot: /home/tk185141.linux/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 1
  runRoot: /run/user/1640274643/containers
  volumePath: /home/tk185141.linux/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

  APIVersion: 3.0.0
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970

  GoVersion: go1.16
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.0.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
tk185141@lima-default:/Users/tk185141$ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/hirsute,now 3.0.1+dfsg1-1ubuntu1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**
Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Lima-VM and Podman

comments.body:
@AkihiroSuda  This did resolve that problem and now hitting a guid and uid issue.
```
WUSTK185141-CV3:test tk185141$ podman-remote build .
Error: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 1640274643:1474848042 for /var/tmp/libpod_builder695095937/build/Dockerfile): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /var/tmp/libpod_builder695095937/build/Dockerfile: invalid argument
```

The actual Dockerfile is quite simple
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/66785012/132856081-f9a9af9f-10e1-414f-a5d8-92e793c1f2fc.png)

I attempted to follow this article but did not seem to work (actually failed to run podman)

https://opensource.com/article/18/12/podman-and-user-namespaces

- First attempt is what is in the article
- Second attempt is what you shared previously
- 

```
WUSTK185141-CV3:~ tk185141$ limactl shell podman podman run -it --uidmap 0:100000:50000 --rm -v $HOME:$HOME:ro docker.io/library/alpine
Error: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 100000:100000 for /home/tk185141.linux/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: chown /home/tk185141.linux/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l: invalid argument
exit status 125
WUSTK185141-CV3:~ tk185141$ limactl shell podman podman run -it --rm -v $HOME:$HOME:ro docker.io/library/alpine
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a0d0a0d46f8b done  
Copying config 14119a10ab done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```
If you run the system/rootful podman (`/run/podman/podman.sock`) instead of the user/rootless podman, you don't get these uid/gid limitations but something that is more similar to (default) Docker.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@tk185141 please reopen on lima if this is till an issue.
